Farn PolrrrcAt- Pnacrrces C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 c 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
April29,2002
Edward C. Noonan
Candidate for Secretary of State
l56l N. Beale Road
Marysville, CA 96901
Re: Our File No. G-02-107
Dear Mr. Noonan
This is in response to your letter dated Apr112,2002, which was received in our
office on April 22,2002. You have requested a hearing by the Fair Political Practices
Commission (the "Commission") regarding a late filing penalty of $100 imposed by the
Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder (the "County") in connection with the filing of
your candidate campaign statement (Form 470).
The Commission is the agency responsible for administering the Political Reform
Act of 1974 (the"Act"), which is contained in title 9 of the California Government Code,
sections 81000-91014. The provisions quoted in your letter are part of the Act. As you
noted, section 91001(a) provides that the Attorney General has responsibility for
enforcement under the Act; however, because section 91001(a) refers to criminal
violations of the Act, it is not applicable to the late filing penalty imposed by the County.l
Section 91013 authorizes the Secretary of State, as well as city and county filing
officers, to impose penalties on candidates and committees that file campaign statements
after their deadline. As you point out, section 84215 requires candidates for statewide
elective office to file their original campaign statements with the Secretary of State, and
copies with the Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles County and the Registrar of Voters of
the City and County of San Francisco.2 According to the letter sent to you by Los
Angeles County, your campaign statement, which was due on January 24,2002, was not
1 The Commission is the civil prosecutor under the Act, and also may impose administrative
penalties. (Sections 83116 and 91001(b).)
2 These requirements are published on the Commission's web site (wwwfppc.ca.gov) as well as
the information manual for candidates (copy enclosed).
ry4


Edward C. Noonan
Our File No. G-02-107
Page2
filed with them until March 21,2002. As a result, the County imposed a late filing
penalty under section 91013(b).
Section 91013 states
"(a) If any person files an original statement or report after any
deadline imposed by this act,he or she shall, in addition to any
other penalties or remedies established by this act, be liable in the
amount of ten dollars ($10) per day after the deadline until the
statement or report is filed, to the officer with whom the statement
or report is required to be filed. Liability need not be enforced by
the filing officer if on an impartial basis he or she determines that
the late filing was not willful and that enforcement of the liability
will not further the purposes of the act, except that no liability shall
be waived if a statement or report is not filed within 30 days for a
statement of economic interest, other than a candidate's statement
filed pursuantto Section 87201, five days for a campaign
statement required to be filed 12 days before an election, and 10
days for all other statements or reports, after the filing officer has
sent specific written notice of the filing requirement.
(b) If any personfiles a copy o/a statement or report afier any
deodline imposed by this act, he or she shall, in addition to any
other penalties or remedies established by this chapter, be liable in
the amount of ten dollars ($10) per day, starting l0 days, orfive
days in the case of a campaign statement required to befiled I2
days before an election, after the fficer has sent specific written
notice of the filing requirement and until the statement isfiled.
(c) The officer shall deposit any funds received under this
section into the general fund of the jurisdiction of which he or she
is an officer. No liability under this section shall exceed the
cumulative amount stated in the late statement or report, or one
hundred dollars ($100), whichever is greater."
(Emphasis added.)
The Act grants total authonty for imposing or waiving late filing penalties to the
filing officer. There is no provision for an appeal or hearing before the Commission or
the Attorney General, and the Commission has no authority under the Act to rescind the
fine or direct the County to rescind the fine. (Owens Advice Letter, No. G-94- 15 1;
Burgess Advice Letter, No. 4-98-245.) You may wish to contact the County to
determine if there are any procedures under which you may request from them a waiver
or reduction of the fine.


Edward C. Noonan
Our File No. G-02-107
Page 3
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please contact me at our toll-free
number (866) 275-3772.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
toL Lr)r'^,t,L-..,
it
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure

sections.question: You have requested a hearing by the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") regarding a late filing penalty of $100 imposed by the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder (the "County") in connection with the filing of your candidate campaign statement (Form 470).
sections.conclusion: The Act grants total authority for imposing or waiving late filing penalties to the filing officer. There is no provision for an appeal or hearing before the Commission or the Attorney General, and the Commission has no authority under the Act to rescind the fine or direct the County to rescind the fine. (Owens Advice Letter, No. G-94-151; Burgess Advice Letter, No. 4-98-245.) You may wish to contact the County to determine if there are any procedures under which you may request from them a waiver or reduction of the fine.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC hear an appeal and rescind or modify a $100 late filing penalty imposed by the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder for a candidate's late campaign statement filing?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC lacks authority to hear appeals or rescind late filing penalties imposed by county filing officers. Under section 91013, only the filing officer may impose or waive such penalties, and the applicant must contact the County directly to request a waiver or reduction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear advice letter format with question and conclusion sections. Document follows standard FPPC response structure. Penalty imposed by county was $100; liability calculated at $10/day from Jan 24 to Mar 21, 2002 (57 days), capped at $100 per section 91013(c). References prior precedential advice letters.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have requested a hearing by the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") regarding a late filing penalty of $100 imposed by the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder (the "County") in connection with the filing of your candidate campaign statement (Form 470).

CONCLUSION: The Act grants total authority for imposing or waiving late filing penalties to the filing officer. There is no provision for an appeal or hearing before the Commission or the Attorney General, and the Commission has no authority under the Act to rescind the fine or direct the County to rescind the fine. (Owens Advice Letter, No. G-94-151; Burgess Advice Letter, No. 4-98-245.) You may wish to contact the County to determine if there are any procedures under which you may request from them a waiver or reduction of the fine.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises Edward Noonan, a candidate for Secretary of State, that it has no authority to hear his appeal of a $100 late filing penalty imposed by Los Angeles County for his tardy campaign statement filing. Late filing penalties are within the sole discretion of the county filing officer under section 91013 of the Political Reform Act.
